Unity.meaning: Real-time 3D application development across WebGL simulations, VR training, AR visualization, interactive labs, games, and technical demos.
talkingPoints: Built both polished user-facing experiences and behavior-focused engineering simulations.
Used Unity as a bridge between technical systems, training workflows, and interactive visualization.
Shipped across WebGL, mobile, VR, and prototype contexts.

C#.meaning: Gameplay, simulation, UI, data-flow, and training-logic implementation inside Unity projects.
talkingPoints: Implemented station logic, interaction systems, control workflows, and UI behavior.
Used C# to connect visual feedback with underlying simulation state.

WebGL.meaning: Browser-delivered interactive simulations and demos that make Unity work easier to share publicly.
talkingPoints: Turned technical projects into accessible browser experiences.
Balanced media, UI, and performance constraints for portfolio-safe demos.

Simulation & Digital Twin.meaning: Behavior-driven virtual systems that explain motion, state, sensors, feedback, and training workflows without exposing private implementation files.
talkingPoints: Modeled visible system behavior rather than claiming ownership of physical hardware.
Translated industrial and educational systems into understandable training experiences.

Control Systems.meaning: Interactive learning tools for PID response, tuning, disturbance behavior, and performance analysis.
talkingPoints: Made abstract control-system behavior visible through graphs, parameters, and guided feedback.
Connected engineering theory with student-facing experimentation.

VR / AR.meaning: Immersive and spatial experiences for booths, training, interior visualization, and lab exploration.
talkingPoints: Built experiences for both headset-based presence and mobile AR preview workflows.
Focused on practical interaction design, onboarding, and reliable demonstration flow.

Educational Technology.meaning: Interactive systems that help learners understand engineering, science, environmental, or training concepts through direct manipulation.
talkingPoints: Used visual feedback and guided interaction to make technical topics easier to grasp.
Designed project pages so HR can scan quickly while technical reviewers can go deeper.

Mobile Development.meaning: Mobile-facing Unity work across educational games and AR/interior visualization workflows.
talkingPoints: Handled platform constraints, mobile UI, and device-friendly interaction patterns.

Backend & Cloud.meaning: Supporting services, APIs, hosting, and account/data integration around interactive applications.
talkingPoints: Integrated runtime content, backend services, and public prototype workflows where needed.

Data & ML.meaning: Data-facing project work around monitoring, visualization, and decision-support prototypes.
talkingPoints: Presented data and ML ideas through understandable user workflows and public demos.

UI / Architecture.meaning: Unity UI, state organization, interaction architecture, multiplayer/collaboration support, and reusable systems.
talkingPoints: Kept interaction-heavy projects understandable and maintainable.
Built UI for simulation feedback, debugging, onboarding, and public demos.

Localization.meaning: Localized interface and instruction work for Arabic learning experiences.
talkingPoints: Supported kid-friendly educational flow with localized instructions and readable feedback.

mediumRiskPatterns.label: PLC code detail
mediumRiskPatterns.pattern: \b(TIA Portal|ladder logic|PLC code|function block|data block|I/O address|input address|output address)\b

mediumRiskPatterns.label: exact sensor or component list
mediumRiskPatterns.pattern: \b(capacitive sensor|inductive sensor|reed switch|photoelectric sensor|light barrier|5/2 single solenoid valve|limit switch|stepper motor)\b
